Caption: (a) A depiction of the Surface-scan MRI sensor (front and back side, X-Z view) based on the parallel-plate architecture. The front plate is the working surface of the sensor placed in contact with the pouch cell. Two arrays of capacitors (top and bottom, N C = 10) connect two thin (ca. 60 μm) copper sheets separated by a thin (ca. 1 mm) layer of proton-rich polymer. Variable capacitors for tuning and matching are incorporated on the back side of the sensor. A circuit diagram of the sensor is shown on the right. (b) Local representation of magnetic field perturbations propagating into the detection medium. (c) Surface-scan MRI image (left) of an 'iPhone-5S' pouch cell (right) registered in a 3T field. The image corresponds to an area (ca. 34 × 65 mm2) indicated with a blue rectangle. Strong dipolar patterns form at the tip of the extended Ni-plated tab and at the terminals (indicated with white rectangles).

(a, b) Simulated maps of B 1 field (X-Z view, averaged along the Y axis) for 70 × 70 × 1 and 40 × 70 × 1 mm3 sensor configurations, respectively. N C values are indicated near each image. (c) B 1 distribution parameters depending on N C. Red traces – configuration 70 × 70 × 1 mm3; Blue traces – configuration 40 × 70 × 1 mm3. Top: mean (○) and local (×) B 1 values at the geometric centers of the models; Bottom: (□) root-mean-square deviations (σ).

(a) Photographs of a solenoidal sensor prototype (top) and the 'iPhone-6' cell placed in contact with the sensor (bottom). (b) T 2*-weighted B 1 - modulated density image of the silicone detection medium. (c,d) Maps of B 1 distribution inside the solenoidal sensor without and with the pouch cell attached, respectively. (e) Magnetic field perturbations at the surface of the 'iPhone-6' pouch cell in the 3T field. ROIs shown in (b-e) correspond to an area ca. 71 × 71 mm2.

Photographs of the 'W' N C = 18 (a) and 'N' N C = 18 (b) sensor configurations. Left: working surface; Middle: backside containing tuning and matching capacitors; Right: 'One Plus 7 Pro' (a) and 'iPhone-6' (b) battery cells placed in contact with the working surfaces of the sensors.

(a-d) Maps of B 1 distribution (Eq. (5)) inside 'W' (N C = 6, 18, and 34), and 'N' (N C = 18) sensor configurations, respectively. (e-h) Magnetic field perturbations (Eq. (7)) produced by 'One Plus 7 Pro' and 'iPhone-6' pouch cells (as indicated above each map) measured with 'W' (N C = 6, 18, and 34), and 'N' (N C = 18) sensors, respectively. ROIs correspond to an area of 71 × 71 mm2.
(a) B 1 map of the 'W' (N C = 34) sensor in contact with the 'One Plus 7 Pro' cell (b). B 1 data are corrupted near the tips of the extended tab and terminals indicated with red rectangles in (b).

B DC maps of a fully charged 'iPhone-5S' cell operating at a current magnitude of 2 A (left - charging; right - discharging). Measurements were performed using the 'N' (N C = 18) sensor. The position of ROI (34 × 65 mm2) with respect to the cell is indicated with a blue rectangle on the right. Positions of the terminals and the extended tab are highlighted with white rectangles.
